reason: Closed-book unsolvable/ambiguous: options A and E rely on paper-specific mechanistic claims (SiOx volume-change cracking causing premature SEI decomposition; inference about faster heat accumulation rate) that are not provided in the stem and cannot be derived from the given temperatures by general reasoning alone. Option F also depends on a separator shutdown temperature (~144°C) that is not stated in the stem. Thus the correct/incorrect labeling cannot be validated without hidden literature details.

MCQ with papers provided. The stem’s first paragraph asserts 30% SOC at 2 mm/s has substantially lower peak temperature than 90% SOC, but the second paragraph asserts 30% SOC at 0.5 mm/s reaches ~800°C; this makes option A effectively a restatement of given facts, yet option G also becomes trivially true, creating non-unique/low-discrimination correctness under closed-book reasoning. More importantly, option C’s correctness depends on an unstated electrical model linking measured voltage drop during an internal short to driving force vs short-circuit resistance; with only the stem, higher voltage drop could also be consistent with higher short resistance under different measurement interpretations, so it is not uniquely decidable closed-book (ambiguity/hidden-assumption dependency).

MCQ with papers provided. All Correct-labeled options (A, B, F, G) are solvable from the stem plus general reasoning without needing unstated paper-only details, and they match the evidence: electrode-only TR implies ISC not required as major heat source (A); ARC surface temperature can undercount internal heat vs DSC (B); cathode phase transitions at 300/400°C occur after ARC-defined TTR=188°C so are progression not initiation (F); scalar heat discrepancy cannot uniquely determine internal temperature profile (G). No fatal ambiguity: the Incorrect options (C, D, E) are not defensible under the stem’s explicit TTR definition, surface-measurement limitation, and lack of mechanistic basis for SOC threshold.

Short-answer; papers provided. The stem contains all necessary numerical values (Tonset, Ttr/TTR ranges) and explicitly states the graphite-cell SEI decomposition–regeneration balance window, while the evidence bundle directly supports the required SiOx-specific mechanism (volume-change-induced uneven SEI with cracks/voids accelerating premature SEI decomposition) and the Tonset/TTR values for both cells. The gold answer’s claims are derivable from the stem plus general mechanistic reasoning and are not contradicted by the evidence. Non-ambiguity is acceptable because the question asks for evaluation and an additional mechanism explicitly anchored in provided evidence.
